page: Microsoft Azure F64ads_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Fadsv7 family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz, featuring 64 physical cores and 64 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server includes a bundled 7559 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The server achieves top 10% placement in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as a PassMark CPU score of 114,652.97. Memory performance is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached bandwidth reaching 11,936.42 GB/sec and large block uncached write bandwidth at 205.45 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are also in the top tier, highlighted by 170.73 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and a static web serving throughput of 99.57 GB/sec.

While the instance excels at CPU-bound tasks and small-to-medium model inference, performance drops to average levels for large-scale workloads like 70B LLM inference due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. This hardware profile represents a highly resource-dense, cost-efficient solution for high-throughput general-purpose workloads, database operations, web serving, and data decompression tasks that require substantial local storage and high-frequency CPU performance without the premium cost of GPU-accelerated instances.
description: Microsoft Azure F64ads_v7 is a high-performance, x86_64 architecture server from the Fadsv7 family, powered by a dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 7559 GB local storage disk, operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Lacking a GPU, it relies on its high-frequency CPU and memory architecture, delivering top-tier results in PassMark CPU tests, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. While highly efficient for CPU-intensive tasks, database operations, and small-to-medium language model inference, its performance drops to average levels for large 70B LLM workloads. This instance offers a cost-effective, resource-dense profile for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads, high-throughput web serving, and database management requiring large local storage capacities.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F64ads_v7 is a 64-core AMD EPYC server with 256 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, delivering top-tier CPU and database performance for demanding general-purpose and storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F64ads_v7 is a 64-vCPU AMD EPYC server featuring 256 GB RAM and 7559 GB local storage, optimized for high-throughput database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency 64-core AMD EPYC server featuring massive local storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
256 GB of system memory paired with 7559 GB of local storage
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis SET benchmarks
Excellent throughput for static web serving and data decompression
Average performance on large 70B LLM workloads due to lack of GPU
Best suited for high-throughput databases, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications
